text:
Code refactoring is the process of improving the structure of existing code without changing its behavior. When developers refactor, they make the code cleaner, easier to read, and more maintainable. For example, they might rename confusing variables, break long functions into smaller ones, or remove duplicate logic.

Refactoring is important because it helps reduce technical debt. Technical debt is when code becomes messy or hard to work with over time, like financial debt that must be paid eventually. By regularly refactoring, teams keep their codebase healthy, reduce bugs, and make it faster for new developers to understand the project. The key rule is always: improve the structure, but don't change what the code does.
